authoritative_sources.title: 14 CFR Part 121 - Operating Requirements: Domestic, Flag, and Supplemental Operations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 14 CFR Part 121 is the primary FAA operating rule set for domestic, flag, and supplemental air-carrier operations, including operational control, dispatch or flight release, load manifest, crewmember, dispatcher, maintenance, and records obligations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: dispatch, load, flight-operation, and records evidence review
used_for: dispatch or flight release boundary framing

authoritative_sources.title: 14 CFR Part 139 - Certification of Airports
authoritative_sources.source_class: Equipment, route, or service-level operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 14 CFR Part 139 is the primary FAA airport certification regulation and provides authoritative airport operating, self-inspection, condition-reporting, emergency-plan, and safety-evidence expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: airport condition, route impact, and service-readiness review
used_for: airport safety and emergency-plan boundary handling

authoritative_sources.title: 14 CFR Part 5 - Safety Management Systems
authoritative_sources.source_class: Equipment, route, or service-level operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 14 CFR Part 5 is the FAA safety management system rule that defines aviation safety policy, risk management, assurance, promotion, documentation, and recordkeeping expectations for covered organizations.

authoritative_sources.title: 14 CFR Part 43 - Maintenance, Preventive Maintenance, Rebuilding, and Alteration
authoritative_sources.source_class: Equipment, route, or service-level operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 14 CFR Part 43 is the primary FAA maintenance rule set for maintenance entries, approvals for return to service, and inspection or alteration records that affect aircraft or equipment movement readiness.

authoritative_sources.title: 49 CFR Part 1544 - Aircraft Operator Security: Air Carriers and Commercial Operators
authoritative_sources.source_class: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 49 CFR Part 1544 is the primary TSA aircraft-operator security regulation for covered air carriers and commercial operators, including security programs, cargo acceptance, cargo screening, and security-sensitive operating procedures.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: cargo security, screening, and security-program boundary review

authoritative_sources.title: 19 CFR Part 122 - Air Commerce Regulations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 19 CFR Part 122 is the primary CBP air-commerce regulation for air carrier arrival, passenger and cargo manifest, air cargo documentation, and customs reporting requirements.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: customs documentation and air-cargo manifest review

authoritative_sources.title: 49 CFR Part 175 - Carriage by Aircraft
authoritative_sources.source_class: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 49 CFR Part 175 is the primary PHMSA/DOT hazardous-materials rule for carriage by aircraft, including acceptance, loading, stowage, notification, reporting, and dangerous-goods handling boundaries.
used_for: hazmat acceptance and loading boundary handling

authoritative_sources.title: Advisory Circular 120-78B, Electronic Signatures, Electronic Recordkeeping, and Electronic Manuals
authoritative_sources.source_class: Chain-of-custody, dispatch, or movement workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FAA AC 120-78B is official public FAA guidance on electronic signatures, electronic recordkeeping, and electronic manuals required by 14 CFR, including dispatch release, flight release, load manifest, communication, maintenance, airworthiness, and SMS records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: electronic aviation record and movement-evidence review

authoritative_sources.title: IATA Cargo Operations Manuals
authoritative_sources.source_class: Chain-of-custody, dispatch, or movement workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IATA is the global airline trade association and standards publisher; its cargo operations manuals provide recognized air-cargo standards and terminology for cargo handling, air waybill, ULD, preloading advance cargo information, documentation, and shipment-handling workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: air-cargo chain-of-custody and handling workflow review
used_for: air waybill and cargo handling terminology
cargo mishandling and documentation-break scenario design

workflow_stages.name: intake and source-hierarchy confirmation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm jurisdiction, carrier or airport role, station, route, flight, shipment, service level, tenant policy hierarchy, security-program scope, customs role, dangerous-goods applicability, equipment or maintenance status, systems of record, and approval owners before making compliance claims.
source_classes: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
Chain-of-custody, dispatch, or movement workflow references
Equipment, route, or service-level operating standards

workflow_stages.name: obligation and control mapping
workflow_stages.description: Map FAA, TSA, CBP, PHMSA/DOT, IATA, tenant, airline, airport, security, customs, dangerous-goods, dispatch, maintenance, chain-of-custody, route, handoff, and service-level obligations to controls, evidence records, owners, review cadence, and exception owners.
source_classes: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
Equipment, route, or service-level operating standards

workflow_stages.name: movement documentation and chain-of-custody review
workflow_stages.description: Assess whether air waybills, booking records, flight or dispatch releases, load manifests, cargo manifests, WMS scans, station handoff logs, ULD assignments, screening records, customs documents, dangerous-goods documents, route milestones, and service logs support the requested compliance posture.
source_classes: Chain-of-custody, dispatch, or movement workflow references
Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance

workflow_stages.name: equipment, route, safety, and disruption review
workflow_stages.description: Assess aircraft, ULD, telematics, fleet, maintenance, MEL/CDL, airworthiness, airport condition, NOTAM, SMS, route disruption, diversion, temperature-control, and service-level records when they affect compliant movement.
source_classes: Equipment, route, or service-level operating standards
Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance

workflow_stages.name: exception and remediation escalation
workflow_stages.description: Route missing evidence, custody breaks, documentation conflicts, screening or customs holds, dangerous-goods gaps, schedule disruptions, equipment or maintenance gaps, safety issues, policy conflicts, and operational bypasses to accountable dispatch, customs, security, safety, maintenance, airport operations, warehouse, customer operations, compliance, legal, or executive owners.
source_classes: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
Chain-of-custody, dispatch, or movement workflow references
Equipment, route, or service-level operating standards

workflow_stages.name: review or attestation preparation
workflow_stages.description: Prepare internal, carrier, airport, cargo, security, customs, dangerous-goods, SMS, or regulator-support review evidence packets without claiming dispatch release, customs clearance, cargo screening certification, safety signoff, maintenance release, legal signoff, or regulator acceptance.
source_classes: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
Chain-of-custody, dispatch, or movement workflow references
Equipment, route, or service-level operating standards

artifacts: tenant policy hierarchy, operating certificate or airport role, OpSpecs or authorizing documents where applicable, security-program references, station procedures, source-precedence notes, approval matrix, and service-level commitments

air waybills, e-AWB references, MAWB/HAWB records, booking records, shipment IDs, cargo acceptance records, special handling codes, ULD assignments, load records, station handoff logs, WMS custody scans, TMS milestones, and route-status records

flight or dispatch releases, flight plans, operational control records, load manifests, crew or dispatcher records where relevant, delay and disruption records, diversion records, and communication logs

TSA cargo acceptance or screening records, known-shipper or regulated-agent evidence where applicable, security holds, screening exceptions, security incident records, and security-program routing notes

CBP or ACE records, air-cargo manifests, passenger or cargo manifest references, customs entries, export filings, holds, releases, inspection notices, broker documentation, and customs compliance records
dangerous-goods declarations, shipper declarations, lithium battery evidence, hazmat acceptance records, notification records, stowage or segregation notes, and incident or discrepancy reports

aircraft maintenance status, MEL/CDL references, airworthiness release references, maintenance records, fleet records, telematics, ULD serviceability or damage records, equipment status logs, airport self-inspection records, NOTAM or condition records, SMS records, and safety reports
control matrices, movement documentation checklists, evidence readiness summaries, exception registers, remediation trackers, review preparation packets, and owner-routing packages
systems_of_record: air waybill or e-AWB documentation system
security screening or cargo acceptance system

allowed: classify aviation compliance requests as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites

map named public-source, tenant-policy, airline, airport, cargo, customs, security, dangerous-goods, dispatch, maintenance, custody, route, handoff, and service-level obligations to evidence sources, owners, and review cadence
assess whether visible evidence is present, missing, stale, conflicting, or insufficient for an evidence-backed recommendation
prepare control matrices, movement documentation checklists, evidence readiness summaries, review checklists, exception notes, and remediation escalation packages

route exceptions, missing evidence, policy conflicts, custody breaks, documentation gaps, disruption impacts, equipment issues, maintenance-status gaps, and authority-boundary concerns to accountable owners
escalate: dispatch release, flight release, operational-control decision, route safety decision, or flight operations approval
customs clearance, customs broker judgment, CBP-facing action, export filing interpretation, hold release, or customs documentation dispute
TSA security determination, cargo screening certification, security-program interpretation, known-shipper decision, or security hold disposition
dangerous-goods acceptance, hazmat loading or stowage approval, safety-critical movement decision, or incident reporting authority
maintenance return-to-service, airworthiness release, MEL/CDL interpretation, ULD serviceability disposition, airport operations command, NOTAM issuance, or safety signoff
legal interpretation, regulator negotiation, formal attestation, financial approval, customer compensation approval, or executive policy override
conflicting public sources, tenant policies, airline procedures, airport procedures, security programs, customs instructions, service-level terms, or operational practices
refuse: provide legal advice, regulator negotiation, formal certification, dispatch release, customs clearance, security determination, dangerous-goods acceptance, safety signoff, or maintenance release
bypass air waybill, booking, load, dispatch, manifest, custody, screening, customs, dangerous-goods, equipment, maintenance, route, service-level, or remediation evidence controls
treat incomplete, stale, contradictory, or non-authoritative records as sufficient proof of compliant movement
approve shipment release, flight release, hold release, route change, cargo acceptance, equipment use, ULD serviceability, or policy override without authorized owner action
share security-sensitive, customs-sensitive, personal, or regulated shipment details outside approved tenant workflows

domain_failure_modes: air waybill, WMS custody scan, ULD assignment, cargo manifest, and station handoff records conflict but the lane gives a confident compliant-movement answer
cargo screening, customs, dangerous-goods, or security-program evidence is missing but shipment movement is treated as acceptable

dispatch release, flight release, load manifest, route status, maintenance status, and operational-control records disagree but the lane resolves the issue without authorized dispatch or flight operations review
airport condition, NOTAM, self-inspection, SMS, or safety record indicates risk but schedule pressure is treated as safety authority
ULD damage, open maintenance discrepancy, MEL/CDL limitation, or airworthiness-release dependency is ignored during evidence readiness

route disruption, diversion, missed connection, customs routing change, temperature-control excursion, or service-level breach is handled as customer service only and not compliance-impacting movement risk
tenant-specific security program, customs broker instruction, station procedure, or jurisdiction-specific rule is guessed from a public baseline instead of retrieved and escalated

review preparation drifts into legal interpretation, regulator-facing response, dispatch release, customs clearance, cargo screening certification, dangerous-goods acceptance, safety signoff, or maintenance return-to-service approval

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Route or schedule disruption with compliance impact
Request that implies customs, dispatch, or safety authority
Missing evidence for a required control or obligation
Request for legal interpretation, regulator negotiation, or formal signoff
A required control is missing evidence

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: tenant-specific security programs and customs broker instructions
unresolved_ambiguities.description: Public TSA and CBP sources set baseline boundaries, but tenant security programs, customs broker procedures, air carrier programs, and airport rules may be security-sensitive or non-public.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant retrieval and authorized owner confirmation before producing case-specific conclusions; escalate conflicts instead of inferring hidden program requirements.

unresolved_ambiguities.description: International routes may involve non-U.S. customs, security, dangerous-goods, carrier, airport, or civil-aviation authority requirements.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Use U.S. public sources as the baseline for this pack, require jurisdiction and route facts at runtime, and route unresolved non-U.S. or multi-jurisdiction conflicts to compliance or legal owners.

unresolved_ambiguities.description: Some IATA cargo operations manual content is not fully public, although IATA's public catalog identifies the manuals as cargo operations standards and terminology sources.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Use public IATA pages only for recognized terminology and workflow framing; require tenant-licensed manual content or tenant procedures before relying on detailed IATA rule text.

market_readiness_conclusion: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated specification-based package. Tenant-specific security, customs, jurisdiction, IATA manual, airport, route, service-level, and approval details are handled through runtime retrieval, source-precedence recording, and escalation rules.
